info.name: Blockchain Simulator v1
info.description:
Complete REST API collection for the Blockchain Simulator.

## Folders
| Folder | Responsibility |
|--------|---------------|
| 🔌 Node Status | Root endpoint — node metadata and route index |
| 🔐 Auth | Register, activate, login, profile |
| 👛 Wallets | Create wallets, signed transfers |
| 🛡 Admin | User + wallet administration, minting |
| ⛏ Mining | Mine blocks via Proof-of-Work |
| 📋 Mempool | Submit and inspect pending transactions |
| 🔗 Chain | Read and validate the blockchain |
| 🌐 Peer Network | Register nodes and run longest-chain consensus |
| 📊 Observability | Health checks and operational metrics |

## Setup
1. Import `blockchain-simulator.postman_environment.json`.
2. Select **Blockchain Simulator — Local** as the active environment.
3. Start the server: `python basic-blockchain.py`.
4. Run the **🔌 Node Status / API Index** request to verify connectivity.

## Auto-populated variables
Requests with test scripts automatically store values into environment variables:
- `activation_code`, `access_token`, `user_id` — set after auth requests.
- `last_block_index`, `last_proof` — set after each mine.
- `chain_length` — set after Get Chain.
- `pending_tx_count` — set after Get Pending Transactions.
- `chain_is_valid` — set after Validate Chain.
- `consensus_replaced` — set after Resolve Consensus.

exec: // Inject a unique request ID into every request

item.name: 🔌 Node Status
item.description:
Root endpoint returning node metadata and the full route index.
Use this as a connectivity check before running any other request.

request.description:
Returns the node description and the list of all available routes.

**Use this first** to verify the server is reachable and to discover endpoint paths.

Expected response keys: `node`, `version`, `routes`.

item.name: ⛏ Mining
item.description:
Mine new blocks using Proof-of-Work.

**Rate limit:** 5 requests per 60 seconds (sliding window).

Mining automatically:
- Flushes all pending mempool transactions into the new block.
- Broadcasts `block_mined` to all WebSocket subscribers.
- Triggers consensus resolution on all registered peers (fire-and-forget).

request.description:
Executes Proof-of-Work and appends a new block to the chain.

The server finds `new_proof` such that `SHA256(new_proof² − prev_proof²)` starts with `DIFFICULTY_PREFIX` (default `00000`).

All pending transactions from the mempool are included and the mempool is cleared.

**Side effects:**
- WebSocket push to all subscribers.
- `GET /nodes/resolve` fired on all registered peers.

**Rate limit:** HTTP 429 after 5 calls within 60 s.

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description:
Register, activate, log in, and fetch the current profile.

Auth endpoints are public except for /auth/me, which requires a Bearer token.

request.description:
Create a new user and receive an activation code.

The activation code is shown only once on this response.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Activate the account using the one-time activation code.
Login and store the JWT for authenticated requests.
Fetch the current authenticated profile.

item.name: 👛 Wallets
item.description:
Wallet creation, listing, and signed transfers.

All endpoints require a Bearer token.

request.description: Create a new wallet and store the returned wallet_id and mnemonic.
Submit a signed transfer. Use a valid signature generated from the sender wallet's mnemonic.

item.name: 🛡 Admin
item.description:
Admin-only user and wallet management.

All endpoints require a Bearer token with the proper permissions.

request.description: Requires MINT permission (not in ADMIN baseline by default).

item.name: 📋 Mempool
item.description:
Manage pending transactions before they are included in a block.

Transactions submitted here enter the **mempool** (pending state). They become confirmed only when a block is mined.

Propagation to registered peers happens automatically (loop prevention via `X-Propagated: 1`).

request.description:
Submits a new transaction to the mempool.

**Business rules enforced (BR-TX-01 to BR-TX-08):**
- `sender` must be non-empty.
- `receiver` must be non-empty.
- `sender ≠ receiver`.
- `amount > 0`.

On success the transaction is stored locally and forwarded to all registered peers (with `X-Propagated: 1` to prevent loops).

Demonstrates BR-TX-04: sender and receiver must differ.
Expected response: **400 VALIDATION_ERROR**.

Demonstrates BR-TX-01: amount must be greater than zero.
Expected response: **400 VALIDATION_ERROR**.

Returns all transactions currently in the mempool (non-destructive — does not clear them).

The mempool is emptied atomically when a block is mined.

item.name: 🔗 Chain
item.description:
Read and validate the blockchain.

The chain is a linked list of blocks where each block references the SHA-256 hash of its predecessor. Tamper detection is performed by re-computing hashes and verifying Proof-of-Work on every block.

request.description:
Returns the complete blockchain in ascending block index order.

Useful for inspecting current state or feeding the data to an external validator.

Runs full chain integrity verification:
1. For each block (from index 2 onward): verifies `SHA256(proof² − prev_proof²)` starts with `DIFFICULTY_PREFIX`.
2. Verifies `previous_hash` matches the SHA-256 of the predecessor block.

Returns `valid: true` only when all checks pass.

item.name: 🌐 Peer Network
item.description:
Register peer nodes and run the longest-chain consensus algorithm.

**How peer networking works:**
1. Register one or more peer URLs (`POST /nodes/register`).
2. Transactions submitted locally are forwarded to all peers automatically.
3. After each mine, peers are notified to run `GET /nodes/resolve` (fire-and-forget).
4. `GET /nodes/resolve` fetches each peer's chain and replaces the local chain if a longer valid one is found (longest-chain rule, BR-CS-01).

request.description:
Registers one or more peer node URLs.

**Normalisation (BR-ND-01):** URLs without a scheme get `http://` prepended; path and query components are stripped.

**Deduplication (BR-ND-02):** A URL already registered is silently ignored.

Change `peer_url` in the environment to match your second node's address.

exec: pm.test('Response has nodes list and total', () => {

request.description: Registers multiple peers in a single call. `localhost:5003` will be normalised to `http://localhost:5003` (BR-ND-01).
Returns all registered peer node URLs in sorted order.

Runs the **longest-chain consensus** algorithm (BR-CS-01):
1. Fetches `/api/v1/chain` from every registered peer (timeout: 5 s).
2. Validates each remote chain.
3. Replaces the local chain with the longest valid one found.

`replaced: true` means the local chain was overwritten.
`replaced: false` means the local chain was already the longest.

response.name: 200 OK — replaced

item.name: 📊 Observability
item.description:
Monitor the health and operational metrics of this node.

These endpoints are safe to call frequently — they are read-only and not rate-limited.

Integrate with Prometheus, Grafana, or any HTTP-based alerting system by polling `/health` and `/metrics`.

request.description:
Returns node health.

| Scenario | HTTP status | `db` field |
|----------|------------|------------|
| In-memory mode | 200 | `"n/a"` |
| PostgreSQL reachable | 200 | `"ok"` |
| PostgreSQL unreachable | 503 | `"error"` |

The DB check uses a 3-second connection timeout with `SELECT 1`.

Returns operational metrics:
- `chain_height` — total number of blocks (including genesis).
- `pending_transactions` — current mempool size.
- `avg_mine_time_seconds` — mean time between consecutive blocks, or `null` if fewer than 2 blocks exist.

item.name: 🧪 Error Cases
item.description:
Requests that exercise the error-handling layer.

All errors return `{"error": "...", "code": "..."}` with the appropriate HTTP status (BR-AP-01). Use these to verify your client handles errors correctly.

request.description: Any unknown path returns HTTP 404 with code `NOT_FOUND`.
Calling `DELETE /chain` returns HTTP 405 because only `GET` is defined.
Sending an incomplete body (missing `receiver` and `amount`) returns HTTP 400 with code `VALIDATION_ERROR`.
